title: Dependabot can't parse your go.mod
body:
Dependabot couldn't parse the go.mod found at `/go.mod`.

The error Dependabot encountered was:

```
go: github.com/containers/buildah@v1.11.6 requires
	github.com/openshift/api@v3.9.1-0.20190810003144-27fb16909b15+incompatible: invalid pseudo-version: preceding tag (v3.9.0) not found
```

[View the update logs](https://app.dependabot.com/accounts/containers/update-logs/18225746).

comments.body: I faced the same issue in my project while running `go mod tidy` today.
Any suggestions for how I might go about fixing this locally?

I also get this error (trying to build libpod for the first time).

Posted on the buildah post as well: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2042
I cannot reproduce that locally. Which version of go are you using?

@vrothberg 

``` 
$ go version
go version go1.13.5 linux/amd64
```

Installed with `yum` on Fedora 31. The error message seems to be related to tags, maybe you don't get it because you have tags from working on the project for a longer time? I guess deleting the dependency in go's github.com folders will cause them to be redownloaded.

> I guess deleting the dependency in go's github.com folders will cause them to be redownloaded.

I did and also tried reproducing in a fresh F31 container but I do not hit the error. I will go ahead an bump the dependency in buildah which should resolve the issue.
This should be fixed with the next update of c/buildah (Cc @TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan )
Yes I want to cut one soon/today?
